title: New Relic Java Agent Configuration
description: Fleet Control configuration schema for the New Relic Java agent. Generated from .fleetControl/schemaGeneration/reference-newrelic.yml.

license_key.description: New Relic license key associated with your account. Binds the agent's data to your account in the New Relic UI.

agent_enabled.description: Agent enabled Use this setting to disable the agent instead of removing it from the startup command. Default is true.

app_name.description: Set the name of your application as you'd like it to show up in the New Relic UI. If enable_auto_app_naming is false, the agent reports all data to this application. Otherwise, the agent reports only background tasks (transactions for non-web applications) to this application. To report data to more than one application (useful for rollup reporting), separate the application names with ";". For example, to report data to "My Application" and "My Application 2" use this: app_name: My Application;My Application 2 This setting is required. Up to 3 different application names can be specified. The first application name must be unique.

high_security.description: To enable high security, set this property to true. When in high security mode, the agent will use SSL and obfuscated SQL. Additionally, request parameters and message parameters will not be sent to New Relic.

enable_auto_app_naming.description: Set to true to enable support for auto app naming. The name of each web app is detected automatically and the agent reports data separately for each one. This provides a finer-grained performance breakdown for web apps in New Relic. Default is false.

enable_auto_transaction_naming.description: Set to true to enable component-based transaction naming. Set to false to use the URI of a web request as the name of the transaction. Default is true.

log_level.description: The agent uses its own log file to keep its logging separate from that of your application. Specify the log level here. This setting is dynamic, so changes do not require restarting your application. The levels in increasing order of verboseness are: off, severe, warning, info, fine, finer, finest Default is info.

audit_mode.description: Log all data sent to and from New Relic in plain text. This setting is dynamic, so changes do not require restarting your application. Default is false.

log_file_count.description: The number of backup log files to save. Default is 1.

log_limit_in_kbytes.description: The maximum number of kbytes to write to any one log file. The log_file_count must be set greater than 1. Default is 0 (no limit).

log_daily.description: Override other log rolling configuration and roll the logs daily. Default is false.

log_file_name.description: The name of the log file. Default is newrelic_agent.log.

datastore_multihost_preference.description: When multiple datastore addresses detected, determines which to use. Values: NONE, FIRST, LAST Default: NONE

legacy_async_api_skip_suspend.description: Workaround for memory leak with legacy async API instrumentation. Default: false

reactor-netty.description: Report Reactor Netty errors. Default: false

distributed_tracing.description: Enable distributed tracing for gRPC calls. Default: true

stats_sampler.description: Enable Hibernate statistics sampler. Default: false

environment_attributes.description: List of environment attributes to include with Kafka config events. Default: empty list

debug.description: Enable debug logging for Kafka metrics. Default: false

as_events.description: Report Kafka metrics as custom events instead of dimensional metrics. Default: false

interval.description: Reporting interval in seconds for Kafka metrics. Default: 30

node.description: Disable collection of Kafka node-level metrics. Default: false

topic.description: Disable collection of Kafka topic-level metrics. Default: false

start_transaction.description: Start a new transaction for HTTP/2 frame read events. Default: false

scala_futures_as_segments.description: Report Scala Futures as transaction segments. Default: false

enabled.description: Enable CallbackRunnable instrumentation for Scala futures. Default: false

send_data_on_exit.description: Send data on JVM exit. Default: false

send_data_on_exit_threshold.description: Seconds app must run before send_data_on_exit applies. Default: 60

sync_startup.description: Wait for New Relic connection on app startup. Default: false

send_environment_info.description: Report JVM settings to New Relic. Default: true

trim_stats.description: Aggregate metrics for segments with <2% of transaction time. Default: true

token_timeout.description: Seconds before async token auto-expires. Default: 180

enabled.description: Provides control over all AI Monitoring functionality. Set as true to enable all AI Monitoring features. Default is false.
Set as false to disable attributes for the input and output content. Default is true.

record_content.description: Provides control over whether attributes for the input and output content should be added to LLM events.

ai_monitoring.description: AI Monitoring captures insights on the performance, quality, and cost of interactions with LLM models made with instrumented SDKs.

enabled.description: Provides control over all the application logging features for forwarding, local log decorating, and metrics features. Set as false to disable all application logging features. Default is true.
When true, application logs will be forwarded to New Relic. The default is true.

auto_app_naming_association.description: When true and "enable_auto_app_naming" is also true, log events will be sent to their corresponding named entity rather than the roll up entity. Default is false.

max_samples_stored.description: Application log events are collected up to the configured amount. Afterwards, events are sampled to maintain an even distribution across the harvest cycle. Default is 10000. Setting to 0 will disable.

enabled.description: When true, custom tags (labels) will be added to agent forwarded logs.

exclude.description: A comma separated list of label keys that should NOT be added to application logs.

labels.description: Provides the ability to add custom tags(labels) to agent forwarded logs. Labels are pulled from the top-level 'labels' section (located near the bottom of this config file). Default is false.

enabled.description: When true, application logs will contain context data.

include.description: A comma separated list of attribute keys whose values should be sent to New Relic.

exclude.description: A comma separated list of attribute keys whose values should not be sent to New Relic.

context_data.description: Whether the log events should include context from loggers with support for that.

log_level_denylist.description: A comma separated list of strings representing log levels that should not be forwarded. Log level names are case-insensitive but otherwise must be an exact match. Example values: "info, debug"

forwarding.description: The agent will automatically forward application logs to New Relic in a format that includes agent metadata for linking them to traces and errors.

enabled.description: When true, application log metrics will be reported. The default is true.

metrics.description: The agent will generate metrics to indicate the number of application log events occurring at each distinct log level.

enabled.description: When true, the agent will decorate your application log files with linking metadata. The default is false.

local_decorating.description: The agent will add linking metadata to each log line in your application log files. This feature should only be used if you want to use a third party log forwarder, instead of the agent's built-in forwarding feature, to send your application log events to New Relic.

application_logging.description: Provides the ability to forward application logs to New Relic, generate log usage metrics, and decorate local application log files with agent metadata for use with third party log forwarders. The application_logging.forwarding and application_logging.local_decorating should not be used together.

enabled.description: When true the agent will capture namespace and function information on spans to enable code level metrics in CodeStream.

code_level_metrics.description: Adds integration with CodeStream, introducing Code-Level Metrics! Golden Signals visible in your IDE through New Relic CodeStream.

proxy_host.description: Proxy settings for connecting to the New Relic server: If a proxy is used, the host setting is required. Other settings are optional. Default port is 8080. The username and password settings will be used to authenticate to Basic Auth challenges from a proxy server. Proxy scheme will allow the agent to connect through proxies using the HTTPS scheme.

account_id.description: AWS account ID for cloud.resource_id attribute.

metadata_bypass_proxy.description: Bypass proxy for cloud metadata endpoints. Default: false

cloud.description: Cloud environment settings for container metadata collection: When running in containerized environments (like AWS Fargate) with a proxy, metadata endpoints may need to bypass the proxy to be accessible.

detect_aws.description: Poll AWS metadata API. Default: true

detect_azure.description: Poll Azure metadata API. Default: true

detect_gcp.description: Poll GCP metadata API. Default: true

detect_kubernetes.description: Report KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST env var. Default: true

detect_pcf.description: Read PCF environment variables. Default: true

detect_docker.description: Read Docker info from file system. Default: true

max_stack_trace_lines.description: Limits the number of lines to capture for each stack trace. Default is 30

enabled.description: When true, attributes will be sent to New Relic. The default is true.

include.description: A comma separated list of attribute keys whose values should be sent to New Relic.

exclude.description: A comma separated list of attribute keys whose values should not be sent to New Relic.

http_attribute_mode.description: Defines which sets of http attributes the agent will send: standard, legacy or both (default). Having the agent send both sets will increase ingestion. Having the agent send only legacy may impact current or future functionality.

attributes.description: Provides the ability to configure the attributes sent to New Relic. These attributes can be found in transaction traces, traced errors, transaction events, and page views.

enabled.description: Enable custom event collection. Default: true

max_samples_stored.description: Max custom events per minute. Default: 30000

enabled.description: Transaction tracer is enabled by default. Set this to false to turn it off. This feature is not available to Lite accounts and is automatically disabled. Default is true.

transaction_threshold.description: Threshold in seconds for when to collect a transaction trace. When the response time of a controller action exceeds this threshold, a transaction trace will be recorded and sent to New Relic. Valid values are any float value, or (default) "apdex_f", which will use the threshold for the "Frustrated" Apdex level (greater than four times the apdex_t value). Default is apdex_f.

sql_metadata_comments.description: Controls the addition of comments to the beginning of executed SQL statements to be used by the New Relic Query Performance Monitoring product for entity linking. Default is false.  A resulting comment would resemble this: /*nr_service_guid=MTE3NDc2MDB8QVBNfAAAAAxJQEEEEE9OfDI4MTc5NDEIEA*/

exec_call_sql_regex_disabled.description: For large SQL statements, executing the regular expressions that attempt to parse exec and call statements can take a significant amount of time. Setting this to true will disable the execution of these complex regular expressions. Default is false.

record_sql.description: When transaction tracer is on, SQL statements can optionally be recorded. The recorder has three modes, "off" which sends no SQL, "raw" which sends the SQL statement in its original form, and "obfuscated", which strips out numeric and string literals. Default is obfuscated.

log_sql.description: Set this to true to log SQL statements instead of recording them. SQL is logged using the record_sql mode. Default is false.

stack_trace_threshold.description: Threshold in seconds for when to collect stack trace for a SQL call. In other words, when SQL statements exceed this threshold, then capture and send to New Relic the current stack trace. This is helpful for pinpointing where long SQL calls originate from. Default is 0.5 seconds.

explain_enabled.description: Determines whether the agent will capture query plans for slow SQL queries. Only supported for MySQL and PostgreSQL. Default is true.

explain_threshold.description: Threshold for query execution time below which query plans will not be captured. Relevant only when `explain_enabled` is true. Default is 0.5 seconds.

top_n.description: Use this setting to control the variety of transaction traces. The higher the setting, the greater the variety. Set this to 0 to always report the slowest transaction trace. Default is 20.

include.description: If attributes are enabled for transaction traces, all attribute keys found in this list will be sent to New Relic in transaction traces.

exclude.description: All attribute keys found in this list will not be sent to New Relic in transaction traces.

attributes.description: This setting can be used to turn on or off all attributes for transaction traces.

transaction_tracer.description: Transaction tracer captures deep information about slow transactions and sends this to the New Relic service once a minute. Included in the transaction is the exact call sequence of the transactions including any SQL statements issued.

enabled.description: This property enables the collection of errors. If the property is not set or the property is set to false, then errors will not be collected. Default is true.

ignore_classes.description: Use this property to exclude specific exceptions from being reported as errors by providing a comma separated list of full class names. The default is to exclude akka.actor.ActorKilledException. If you want to override this, you must provide any new value as an empty list is ignored.

ignore_status_codes.description: Use this property to exclude specific http status codes from being reported as errors by providing a comma separated list of status codes. The default is to exclude 404s. If you want to override this, you must provide any new value because an empty list is ignored.

ignore_messages.description: Exception messages to ignore by class. Example: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: - 'Invalid input*'

expected_classes.description: Expected exception classes (counted but not errors). Array type only.

error_collector.description: Error collector captures information about uncaught exceptions and sends them to New Relic for viewing.

enabled.description: Set to false to disable transaction events. Default is true.

max_samples_stored.description: Events are collected up to the configured amount. Afterwards, events are sampled to maintain an even distribution across the harvest cycle. Default is 2000. Setting to 0 will disable.

include.description: If attributes are enabled for transaction events, all attribute keys found in this list will be sent to New Relic in transaction treventsaces.

exclude.description: All attribute keys found in this list will not be sent to New Relic in transaction events.

attributes.description: This setting can be used to turn on or off all attributes for transaction events.

transaction_events.description: Transaction events are used for histograms and percentiles. Non-aggregated data is collected for each web transaction and sent to the server on harvest.

enabled.description: Set to false to disable distributed tracing. Default is true.

exclude_newrelic_header.description: Agent versions 5.10.0+ utilize both the newrelic header and W3C Trace Context headers for distributed tracing. The newrelic distributed tracing header allows interoperability with older agents that don't support W3C Trace Context headers. Agent versions that support W3C Trace Context headers will prioritize them over newrelic headers for distributed tracing. If you do not want to utilize the newrelic header, setting this to true will result in the agent excluding the newrelic header and only using W3C Trace Context headers for distributed tracing. Default is false.

distributed_tracing.description: Distributed tracing lets you see the path that a request takes through your distributed system. This replaces the legacy Cross Application Tracing feature.

enabled.description: Set to false to disable Span events. Default is true.

max_samples_stored.description: Determines the number of Span events that can be captured during an agent harvest cycle. Increasing the number of Span events can lead to additional agent overhead. A maximum value may be imposed server side by New Relic. Default is 2000

enabled.description: When true, attributes will be sent to New Relic. The default is true.

include.description: A comma separated list of attribute keys whose values should be sent to New Relic.

exclude.description: A comma separated list of attribute keys whose values should not be sent to New Relic.

attributes.description: Provides the ability to filter the attributes attached to Span events. Custom attributes can be added to Span events using the NewRelic.getAgent().getTracedMethod().addCustomAttribute(...) API.

span_events.description: New Relic's distributed tracing UI uses Span events to display traces across different services. Span events capture attributes that describe execution context and provide linking metadata. Span events require distributed tracing to be enabled.

enabled.description: Set to false to disable the thread profiler. Default is true.

thread_profiler.description: Thread profiler measures wall clock time, CPU time, and method call counts in your application's threads as they run. This feature is not available to Lite accounts and is automatically disabled.

auto_instrument.description: By default the agent automatically inserts API calls in compiled JSPs to inject the monitoring JavaScript into web pages. Not all rendering engines are supported. See https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/agents/java-agent/instrumentation/new-relic-browser-java-agent#manual_instrumentation for instructions to add these manually to your pages. Set this attribute to false to turn off this behavior.

tag_lib_instrument.description: For pages that emit the <head> tag via a JSP tag library, enabling this setting will monitor JspWriter print/println calls for output of the <head> element and inject the RUM script automatically.

tag_lib_head_pattern.description: If tag_lib_instrument is true, this is the regex pattern that will be used to detect HTML start head elements. Modify the regex if you have more complex start head elements (attributes for example). The defined pattern is case-insensitive.

enabled.description: Enable attributes for browser monitoring. Default: false

browser_monitoring.description: New Relic Real User Monitoring (RUM) gives you insight into the performance real users are experiencing with your website. This is accomplished by measuring the time it takes for your users' browsers to download and render your web pages by injecting a small amount of JavaScript code into the header and footer of each page.

send_jvm_props.description: By default, the agent sends JVM input arguments to New Relic, where they are visible as Environment data. Set to false to disable the sending of JVM props.

enabled.description: To disable this feature, and send all JVM props unobfuscated, set enabled to false. The default is true.

allow.description: A comma separated list of JVM property names whose values should be sent to New Relic unobfuscated.

block.description: A comma separated list of JVM property names whose values should be sent to New Relic obfuscated.

obfuscate_jvm_props.description: Before sending JVM props to New Relic, the agent will obfuscate all data in a prop after an = sign. For example, the property -Dmy.prop=my-value will be sent to New Relic as -Dmy.prop=obfuscated. By default, the standard and extended JVM props (those beginning with -X*) are sent unobfuscated. Available since agent 8.16.0.

enabled.description: Enable circuit breaker protection. Default: true

memory_threshold.description: Free heap memory percentage threshold. Default: 20

kafka-clients-cluster-metrics.description: This instrumentation emits per-cluster Kafka metrics (MessageBroker/Kafka/Cluster/{id}/...). Requires an AdminClient DescribeCluster call to resolve the cluster ID; opt in when the overhead is acceptable.

classloader_excludes.description: Classes loaded by classloaders in this list will not be instrumented. This is a useful optimization for runtimes which use classloaders to load dynamic classes which the agent would not instrument.

enhanced_spring_transaction_naming.description: Enhanced Spring transaction naming. This feature will name any transaction that originates from a Spring controller after the defined route and HTTP method. For example: "/customer/v1/edit (POST)". This includes controllers that implement or extend interfaces/classes with WebMVC related annotations (@RestController, @Controller, @RequestMapping, etc). By default, this is configured to false, which will name transactions for those types of controllers based on the controller class name and method. For example; "CustomerController/edit". This is the naming logic carried over from previous agent versions. "Standard" controllers, with all relevant annotations present on the actual class, will still get named based on route and HTTP method.

use_controller_class_and_method_for_spri.description: Use controller class and method for Spring transaction naming. When true, all Spring Controller transactions will be named using the controller class name and method name (e.g., "/CustomerController/edit") instead of using request mappings (e.g., "/api/v1/customer (POST)"). This setting takes precedence over enhanced_spring_transaction_naming. This helps prevent transaction name cardinality issues with complex URI patterns. Applies to Spring 4.3.x+ (spring-4.3.0 module) and Spring 6.x+ (spring-6.0.0 module). Default is false.

name_actuator_endpoints.description: By default, built-in actuator endpoints and custom actuator endpoints (using the @Endpoint annotation and it's subclasses) will all be named as "OperationHandler/handle" in New Relic. Setting this to true will result in the transaction name reflecting the actual base actuator endpoint URI. For example, invoking "/actuator/loggers" or "actuator/loggers/com.newrelic" will result in the transaction name "actuator/loggers (GET)". This is to prevent MGI. Default is false.

class_transformer.description: Class transformer can be used to disable all agent instrumentation or specific instrumentation modules. All instrumentation modules can be found here: https://github.com/newrelic/newrelic-java-agent/tree/main/instrumentation

enabled.description: Enable slow SQL tracing. Default: true
Set to true to enable Real-time profiling with JFR. Default is false.

audit_logging.description: Set to true to enable audit logging which will display all JFR metrics and events in each harvest batch. Audit logging is extremely verbose and should only be used for troubleshooting purposes. Default is false.

harvest_interval.description: The time interval, in seconds, of how often JFR data is sent to New Relic. The default is 10 seconds.

queue_size.description: The size of the queue used to store JFR events. Increasing this can reduce gaps in JFR reported data but can also cause resource issues in the agent or cause data to be dropped if backend pipeline limits are exceeded. See: https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/data-apis/ingest-apis/event-api/introduction-event-api/#limits https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/data-apis/ingest-apis/metric-api/metric-api-limits-restricted-attributes/ Default is 250000

use_display_name.description: If true, the JFR host name will be set to the value in the process_host.display_name config. Note that if the process_host.display_name value is empty/null, the default hostname resolution logic will be used. Default is false.

jfr.description: Real-time profiling using Java Flight Recorder (JFR). This feature reports dimensional metric and event data to the endpoints configured by metric_ingest_uri and event_ingest_uri, respectively. Requires a JVM that provides the JFR library.

labels.description: User-configurable custom labels for this agent.  Labels are name-value pairs. There is a maximum of 64 labels per agent.  Names and values are limited to 255 characters. Names and values may not contain colons (:) or semicolons (;).

enabled.description: Set to true to allow individual OpenTelemetry signals to be enabled, false to disable all OpenTelemetry signals. Default is false.

sdk.description: Legacy setting for SDK autoconfigure. Use opentelemetry.enabled instead. Default is false.

enabled.description: Set to true to enable OpenTelemetry Logs signals. Default is true.
Set to true to enable OpenTelemetry Metrics signals. Default is true.

include.description: A comma-delimited string of OpenTelemetry Meters (e.g. "MeterName1,MeterName2") whose signals should be included. By default, all Meters are included. This will override any default Meter excludes in the agent, effectively re-enabling them.

exclude.description: A comma-delimited string of OpenTelemetry Meters (e.g. "MeterName3,MeterName4") whose signals should be excluded. This takes precedence over all other includes/excludes sources, effectively disabling the listed Meters.

export_interval.description: Export interval in milliseconds for OpenTelemetry metrics. Default is 60000 (60 seconds).

export_timeout.description: Export timeout in milliseconds for OpenTelemetry metrics. Default is 10000 (10 seconds).

enabled.description: Set to true to enable OpenTelemetry Traces signals. Default is true.

include.description: A comma-delimited string of OpenTelemetry Tracers (e.g. "TracerName1,TracerName2") whose signals should be included. By default, all Tracers are included. This will override any default Tracer excludes in the agent, effectively re-enabling them.

exclude.description: A comma-delimited string of OpenTelemetry Tracers (e.g. "TracerName3,TracerName4") whose signals should be excluded. This takes precedence over all other includes/excludes sources, effectively disabling the listed Tracers.

opentelemetry.description: Telemetry signals (Logs, Metrics, and Traces) emitted by OpenTelemetry APIs can be incorporated into the Java agent and controlled by the following config options.

enabled.description: Determines whether the security data is sent to New Relic or not. When this is disabled and agent.enabled is true, the security module will run but data will not be sent. Default is false.

mode.description: New Relic Security provides two modes: IAST and RASP Default is IAST. Due to the invasive nature of IAST scanning, DO NOT enable this mode in either a production environment or an environment where production data is processed.

validator_service_url.description: New Relic Security’s SaaS connection URL

agent.description: To completely disable all security functionality, set this flag to false. This property is read only once at application start. Default is false.

iast_scan_request_rate_limit.description: The scan_request_rate_limit configuration allows to specify maximum number of replay request played per minute. The maximum is 3600 and the minimum is 12 replay request per minute.

scan_instance_count.description: This configuration allows users to the number of application instances for a specific entity where IAST analysis is performed.

delay.description: The delay field specifies the delay in minutes before the IAST scan starts. This allows to schedule the scan to start at a later time.

duration.description: The duration field specifies the duration of the IAST scan in minutes. This determines how long the scan will run.

always_sample_traces.description: Allow continuously sample collection of IAST events

scan_schedule.description: The scan_schedule configuration allows to specify when IAST scans should be executed

api.description: The api field specifies list of APIs using regular expression (regex) patterns that follow the syntax of Perl 5. The regex pattern should provide a complete match for the URL without the endpoint. Example: api: - .*account.* - .*/\api\/v1\/.*?\/login

header.description: A list of HTTP header keys. If a request includes any headers with these keys, the corresponding IAST scan will be skipped.

query.description: A list of query parameter keys. The presence of these parameters in the request's query string will lead to skipping the IAST scan.

body.description: A list of keys within the request body. If these keys are found in the body content, the IAST scan will be omitted.

http_request_parameters.description: The parameters configuration allows users to specify headers, query parameters, and body keys that should be excluded from IAST scans. Example: http_request_parameters: header: - X-Forwarded-For query: - username - password body: - account.email - account.contact

iast_detection_category.description: The iast_detection_category configuration allows to specify which categories of vulnerabilities should not be detected by Security Agents. If any of these categories are set to true, Security Agents will not generate events or flag vulnerabilities for that category.

exclude_from_iast_scan.description: The exclude_from_iast_scan configuration allows to specify APIs, parameters, and categories that should not be scanned by Security Agents.

evaluate_completed_transactions.description: If this is set to true, every transaction will be checked for exceeding the defined threshold on transaction completion. Note that this can be computationally expensive since a stack trace is sent with every SlowTransaction event, if a large number of transaction exceed the threshold.

slow_transactions.description: Slow transaction detection will report an event to New Relic ("SlowTransaction") whenever a Transaction's time exceeds the threshold value (in ms). A transaction will only be reported once and by default, only transactions that are in-process during a harvest cycle will be checked. Only the slowest single transaction will be reported even if multiple transactions exceed the threshold.
